title: Agent Control Contract — Management Profile
description: The operator management profile of the Agent Control Contract: the operator MCP tool set served on the management transport, the agent:// operator resources, and the PeerOrigin authority model. Authority: agent RFC 0015 §3.4 (PeerOrigin gating), §4 (operator tools drain/lame-duck/pause/resume/cancel; attach == subagent.send), §5.1 (operator agent:// resources), §5.5 (operator-surface metrics), §7 (trust boundary = transport). Reference source of truth: crates/agent/src/capabilities.rs (OPERATOR_TOOLS const, surfaces() builder, management = bool|String), signals.rs (drain machinery), agent_uri.rs (resource URIs). This is a DATA catalogue describing the profile; tools are advertised in manifest surfaces.operator_tools. Versioning: open objects are additive-tolerant; a new tool/resource is a MINOR bump; a consumer MUST tolerate unknown additive tools/resources and refuse only an unknown MAJOR. A surfaces{} key ABSENT => surface unbuilt => graceful degradation.

operator_tools.description: The operator-tool set advertised on the management transport and mirrored in manifest surfaces.operator_tools (capabilities.rs OPERATOR_TOOLS). FROZEN order. Open registry per-tool (additive tools tolerated). Advertised as [] without the serve-mcp feature (no management transport). `attach` is NOT a tool: it maps to subagent.send (RFC 0005). There is NO `force` tool in v1 (force == a 2nd SIGTERM).

deadline_ms.note: optional; silently CLAMPED to <= drain_timeout_ms, not rejected

drain.machinery: Identical to receiving SIGTERM -> RFC 0011 §4.2 drain state machine; idempotent + monotonic; a clean drain exits 0 (not 143). Backed by signals.rs request_drain().

ready.note: false => flip to NotReady; true => return to Ready iff conditions hold

lame-duck.machinery: Readiness override toward NotReady only; reversible; performs no drain and triggers no exit.

pause.machinery: Fan-control/pause to roots; suspends at a turn boundary. Reflected in agent_paused gauge.
pause.x-p-pause-note: agentctl design record §14 P-pause/§0.6 marked pause/resume DEFERRED, but the reference SOURCE ships them (OPERATOR_TOOLS includes pause/resume; a test asserts presence, 'RFC 0015 §4.3 — shipped'). Prefer source: both are listed. P-pause is satisfied in source; the design record is stale on this point.

resume.machinery: Inverse of pause; resumes turn execution.
resume.x-p-pause-note: See pause; deferred in the design record, shipped in source.

handle.note: "0" or omitted => the root subtree (whole run); sentinel-in-string

cancel.machinery: Instance-scoped wrapper over subagent.cancel; runs the kill ladder.

attach.note: attach/steer is NOT an operator tool; it is the subagent.send work tool used for multi-turn steering. Gated by P-attach-gate for no-puppeting tenants.

force.note: NOT a tool in v1. Forcing a drain past budget == sending a 2nd SIGTERM.

operator_resources.description: Operator agent:// resources. Open registry (additive resources tolerated). Subscribable resources emit notifications/resources/updated{uri} (notify-then-read; no payload in the notification). Only agent://capabilities is also visible on the stdio (work) transport; all others require the management transport.

agent://capabilities.note: the capabilities manifest / Agent Card; the ONLY operator resource also served on stdio

agent://events.note: bounded ring stream; read query ?after=<seq>&level=<lvl>&event=<dotted-prefix,..>; see events.schema.json

agent://metrics.provisional: P4 — Prom 0.0.4 text body + mimeType not yet pinned

peer_origin.description: PeerOrigin gating — reachability IS authority (RFC 0015 §3.4/§7). The transport determines origin; there is NO in-band auth in v1. Closed enum.

gating.description: What each PeerOrigin may invoke. Access control is the TRANSPORT itself (filesystem perms on a unix socket; the VM boundary on vsock). A non-Management caller invoking an operator tool/resource gets METHOD_NOT_FOUND (-32601).

surfaces_control_keys.description: The surfaces{} sub-block keys this control surface owns (RFC 0014 §6.2). SUM TYPES require hand-written deserializers (agentctl RFC 0018 §3.3). additionalProperties:true — a key ABSENT => surface unbuilt => graceful degradation.

operator_tools.note: empty [] without the serve-mcp feature; FROZEN order drain,lame-duck,pause,resume,cancel

events.note: requires the events feature AND a management transport

operator_state_metrics.description: Operator-state metrics (names FROZEN by RFC 0016; this surface only references them — see metrics.registry.json). Neutral agent_* names.

meta_convention.description: The _meta keys this control surface relies on (RFC 0011 §6.2; RFC 0015 §5.6). Neutral agent/* names. traceparent is W3C and unbranded.

x-sum-types.surfaces.management: false | string (vsock:PORT | unix:PATH) — hand-written deserializer required
x-sum-types.surfaces.exit_codes: string version ("1.0"); RFC 0014 §5 sketch showed the "RFC-0011-§5" form, source pins "1.0"
boolean override / sentinel-string "0" / clamped integer respectively

x-provisional.p_pause: RESOLVED-in-source: pause/resume ship despite the design record marking them deferred.
x-provisional.p4_resources: agent://capacity and agent://metrics bodies are undefined upstream (P4); listed but provisional.
x-provisional.operator_metric_divergence: RFC 0015 §5.5 lists gauges agent_draining + agent_lame_duck + agent_paused; the SOURCE emits agent_paused + agent_ready (computed) + agent_drains_total{phase} with NO standalone draining/lame_duck gauge. draining/lame_duck are observable via agent_ready==0 + agent_drains_total. Prefer source.
x-provisional.auth_model: No in-band auth in v1; the transport IS the authority. A future cutover may add credentialed management access (GA decision).
